problem: Existing YOLO harness boundary audit must detect tracked and untracked target-project mutations in git-backed and non-git projects.
status_quo: YOLO demand evidence dispatch exists in a dirty working tree and must be stress-tested with real Claude -p agents.
desired_outcome: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
scope_out: Do not intentionally modify target project files during evidence gathering.
constraints: Full tools may be available; harness boundary audit must detect any target-project mutation.
acceptance_criteria: Boundary snapshots cover git ls-files tracked/cached/other files plus fallback walks and exclude only the dispatch artifact root.
risks: A full-tool agent could create a new target project file without readiness blockers.

actions.reason: Verify blockers and assumptions are not promoted to facts.

protocol.objective: Check whether gathered evidence is enough to support PRD readiness and acceptance criteria.
protocol.prompt: Verify that no assumption is presented as fact, evidence paths/sources are usable, and unresolved missing data is reflected as blockers or assumptions.

agent_results.claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch implements boundary snapshot audit using git ls-files for tracked/cached/other files with walkFiles fallback and excludes only the dispatch artifact root from mutation detection.

evidence.summary: gitFiles runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' to get tracked, cached, and other files
evidence.why: -c flag adds cached files, -o adds other files, covering both tracked and untracked working tree files

evidence.summary: walkFiles provides fallback file walk excluding .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Non-git projects still need boundary audit via filesystem walk

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot uses gitFiles with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowedRoots from snapshot
evidence.why: Allowed artifact roots should not trigger mutation violations

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots compares before/after maps to detect added, deleted, and modified files
evidence.why: Detects any project mutations during agent execution

evidence.summary: Runtime captures boundary snapshots before/after agent dispatch and reports violations
evidence.why: Ensures evidence agents do not mutate target project files

evidence.summary: Test proves git-backed boundary detects untracked mutations via git ls-files -co
evidence.why: Validates that git ls-files -co catches untracked file additions

evidence.summary: Test proves non-git walkFiles fallback detects modifications and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Validates fallback behavior and artifact exclusion

assumptions: Git ls-files -co covers all tracked and untracked working tree files (not verified against git docs)
SHA256 file digest is sufficient for mutation detection (collision risk assumed negligible)
Excluded directories (.git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache) are sufficient for typical projects
risks: Git ls-files flags may not cover all edge cases (untracked directories, submodules, worktrees)
SHA256 collisions could theoretically mask mutations
Excluded directory list may miss project-specific directories that should be ignored
Race conditions: rapid file changes during snapshot could miss mutations

result.notes: Core boundary audit implementation exists and is tested. Git ls-files covers tracked/cached/other files with walkFiles fallback. Artifact root exclusion works. Assumptions around git flag coverage and directory exclusions are reasonable but not externally verified.

agent_results.claim: Explorer evidence is valid: boundary snapshot audit implementation exists with git ls-files coverage, walkFiles fallback, artifact root exclusion, mutation detection, and test coverage.

evidence.summary: gitFiles implementation confirmed: runs 'git ls-files -co --exclude-standard -z' for tracked/cached/other files
evidence.why: Primary evidence supports claim that git-backed boundary audit covers tracked and untracked working tree files

evidence.summary: walkFiles fallback confirmed: excludes .git, node_modules, dist, coverage, .next, .cache
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that non-git projects have fallback file walk with reasonable defaults

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot confirmed: uses gitFiles with walkFiles fallback and excludes allowedRoots
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that artifact roots are excluded from snapshot to prevent false violations

evidence.summary: diffBoundarySnapshots confirmed: compares before/after maps for added/deleted/modified files
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that mutations are detected via snapshot comparison

evidence.summary: Runtime boundary snapshot capture confirmed: before/after agent dispatch with violation reporting
evidence.why: Evidence supports claim that boundary audit is enforced during agent execution

evidence.summary: Git-backed boundary test confirmed: validates untracked mutation detection via git ls-files -co
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms implementation behavior for tracked/untracked files

evidence.summary: Non-git fallback test confirmed: validates walkFiles detects modifications and ignores artifacts
evidence.why: Test evidence confirms fallback behavior and artifact exclusion work as claimed

risks: Git ls-files -co flag behavior not verified against git documentation for edge cases
SHA256 collision risk assumed negligible without cryptanalysis evidence
Excluded directory list may not cover all project-specific build/cache directories
Race condition risk from rapid file changes during snapshot not addressed

result.notes: All 7 evidence records have valid project scope, correct source types, exact paths, line ranges, and relevant summaries. Evidence supports the claim. Explorer correctly identified 4 assumptions and 4 risks rather than promoting them to facts. No evidence conflicts detected. PRD readiness criteria are met: project facts are verified from code and tests, assumptions are clearly separated, and acceptance criteria (boundary snapshots covering git/walk files with artifact exclusion) have evidence.
